notes: Publisher and aggregator of all seven practice-definition templates; January 2000 planning cycle

Aberdeen Group Category Creator: Enterprise Applications and Services (EAS) — credited by Peter S. Kastner. Listed as the lead member of the EAS practice team in the January 2000 EAS Web Description; the EAS practice covered Enterprise Resource Planning (Finance/Accounting, Manufacturing/Logistics, Human Resource Management), eLearning, and IT Professional Services with a four-segment supplier matrix (IPSPs, ISVs, OEMs, Resellers/VARs)

Aberdeen Group analyst on Enterprise Applications and Services (EAS) practice team; lead analyst for e-Employee Self-Service surveys (Supplier Survey June 2000; End-User Competitive Landscape December 2000); HR/HRMS coverage

Aberdeen Group Category Creator: Professional Services Automation (PSA), March 1999 whitepaper 'Professional Services Automation: Increasing Service Organizations' Profitability and Productivity'. Known as the 'Father of PSA'. Founded SPI Research. Listed as Professional Services lead within the Enterprise Applications and Services (EAS) practice in the January 2000 practice definition; primary author of the four-segment IT Professional Services taxonomy (IPSPs, ISVs, OEMs, Resellers/VARs)

Aberdeen Group Category Creator: Enterprise Management (Enterprise Systems Management / Enterprise IT Management) — credited by Peter S. Kastner. Research Director, Enterprise Management practice, Aberdeen Group January 2000. Practice covered systems/network/applications/web operations management; explicitly raised the practice-naming question to Kastner ('Enterprise Management or Enterprise Systems Management... Left to my own devices, I would go with Enterprise Systems Management or even Enterprise IT Management'). Pioneered Web Operations Management as the intersection of Web Enterprise Systems Management and Web Infrastructure

Aberdeen Group Category Creator: Platform Infrastructure (PI) — credited by Peter S. Kastner. Coordinator, Platform Infrastructure practice, Aberdeen Group January 2000. Authored the two surviving versions of the Platform Infrastructure practice definition. PI thesis articulates that infrastructure must deliver scalability, flexibility, Web robustness, ever-increasing workloads, and rapid online upgradeability for 'Internet time' — a 2000-vintage statement of cloud-native principles
Aberdeen Group analyst contributing to the Platform Infrastructure (PI) practice under Wayne Kernochan; storage and storage management coverage; January 2000 practice definition
Aberdeen Group analyst contributing to the Platform Infrastructure (PI) practice under Wayne Kernochan; storage and infrastructure coverage; January 2000 practice definition

Aberdeen Group Category Creator: Platforms Group (server/client architectures) — credited by Peter S. Kastner. Co-coordinator with James Gruener of the Platforms Group practice, Aberdeen Group January 2000. Practice scope: client architectures, server architectures, OS platforms (Windows 2000, Unix, Linux), processor architectures (IA-64/EPIC, RISC, MAJC), interconnect architectures (SMP, NUMA, clustering, partitioning), information appliances. Authored the candid 'Inflection Points 2000' assessment of Y2K aftermath, EPIC, Windows 2000, and Linux ('that annoying, giggling group of penguin lovers')
Aberdeen Group Category Creator: Platforms Group (co-coordinator) — credited by Peter S. Kastner. Co-coordinator with Joyce Becknell of the Platforms Group practice, Aberdeen Group January 2000

Aberdeen Group Category Creator: B-to-B e-Business & Supply Chain Management — credited by Peter S. Kastner. Author of the January 17 2000 practice-definition memo To: Peter Kastner defining the practice as covering Business-to-Business processes for purchasing/procurement, distribution/fulfillment, direct/indirect selling automation; horizontal and vertical analysis. Authored the 'IT'S THE PROCESS, STUPID' strategic-issue framing and the 'e-Business application and Enterprise application markets are the same market — not separate' thesis
